meta.name: PC-PTSD-5 Primary Care PTSD Screen
meta.fullName: Primary Care PTSD Screen for DSM-5
meta.description: The PC-PTSD-5 is a brief 5-item screening tool designed to identify individuals in primary care settings who may have PTSD. Based on DSM-5 diagnostic criteria for PTSD, it assesses five core symptom areas associated with traumatic events.
meta.author: Prins, Bovin, Smolenski et al. (2016)
meta.reference: Prins A, Bovin MJ, Smolenski DJ, et al. The Primary Care PTSD Screen for DSM-5 (PC-PTSD-5): development and evaluation within a veteran primary care sample. J Gen Intern Med. 2016;31(10):1206-1211.
meta.instruction: Sometimes things happen to people that are unusually or especially frightening, horrible, or traumatic. For example, a serious accident or fire, a physical or sexual assault or abuse, an earthquake or flood, a war, seeing someone be killed or seriously injured, or having a loved one die through homicide or suicide. Have you ever experienced this kind of event? If yes, please answer the following questions about your experiences in the past month.

questions.text: Had nightmares about the event(s) or thought about the event(s) when you did not want to?
Tried hard not to think about the event(s) or went out of your way to avoid situations that reminded you of the event(s)?
Been constantly on guard, watchful, or easily startled?
Felt numb or detached from people, activities, or your surroundings?
Felt guilty or unable to stop blaming yourself or others for the event(s) or any problems the event(s) may have caused?

ranges.label: Screen Negative
ranges.description: Your score is below the screening cutoff (3). This does not suggest probable PTSD. However, this does not completely rule out PTSD or other trauma-related concerns.
suggestions: If you have experienced a traumatic event, continue monitoring your emotional well-being
If trauma-related symptoms persist or worsen, consider seeking reassessment
Keep supportive connections with friends and family

ranges.label: Screen Positive
ranges.description: Your score has reached or exceeded the screening cutoff (>=3), suggesting probable PTSD. Further professional assessment is recommended to confirm diagnosis.
suggestions: Seek evaluation from a mental health professional as soon as possible
Trauma-focused CBT (TF-CBT) and EMDR are evidence-based treatments for PTSD
Do not bear this alone; share your feelings with someone you trust
Avoid using alcohol or drugs to cope with symptoms
Maintain regular routines and daily activities
If in severe distress or crisis, call a crisis hotline immediately (e.g., 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline)
